2.reason: [Missing check for NULL return value from fopen; if file doesn't exist or can't be opened, subsequent fgetc leads to undefined behavior. Also, 'ch' should be int to properly compare with EOF.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.25 , reason:  [Missing check for NULL return value from fopen; if file doesn't exist or can't be opened, subsequent fgetc leads to undefined behavior. Also, 'ch' should be int to properly compare with EOF.]

3.reason: [Type of 'ch' should be int, not char, because fgetc returns an int to accommodate all possible char values plus EOF. Using char may cause premature loop termination or infinite loop depending on signedness.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.25 , reason:  [Type of 'ch' should be int, not char, because fgetc returns an int to accommodate all possible char values plus EOF. Using char may cause premature loop termination or infinite loop depending on signedness.]

4.reason: [Because 'ch' is char, the comparison with EOF (which is negative) may never be true if char is unsigned, leading to infinite loop or incorrect behavior. Also, no word parsing or counting logic exists鈥攑rogram just prints file contents.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.25 , reason:  [Because 'ch' is char, the comparison with EOF (which is negative) may never be true if char is unsigned, leading to infinite loop or incorrect behavior. Also, no word parsing or counting logic exists鈥攑rogram just prints file contents.]

5.reason: [This simply echoes the file content instead of counting words in lowercase and outputting frequencies in lexicographical order as required.]
